title: coleocephalocereus aureus subsp. elongatus
intro: Coleocephalocereus aureus subsp. elongatus, also known as the "Golden Pincushion Cactus," is a rare and unique species of cactus in the genus Coleocephalocereus, characterized by its bright yellow spines and elongated stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Coleocephalocereus aureus subsp. elongatus include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and soothing digestive issues, making it a valuable natural remedy. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
